video_title: Blood of Zeus S3 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
10/10 – A Masterpiece of Mythology, Emotion, and Visual Brilliance

Blood of Zeus Season 3 delivers an epic and deeply satisfying conclusion to one of the best animated shows in recent years. As someone who cherishes Greek mythology, I was genuinely impressed by how respectfully and authentically the series handles its source material. It doesn't stray with unnecessary twists or modern agendas — instead, it celebrates the ancient myths with a refreshing level of sincerity and intensity.

The animation is absolutely stunning, especially in the action sequences, and the musical score perfectly enhances the atmosphere of every scene. I especially loved the way each episode had a different intro logo — a small touch that added to the uniqueness and artistic value of the show.

Voice acting was top-notch across the board. Every character felt alive and emotionally grounded. You could truly feel the passion behind the performances, especially during the more dramatic and tragic moments.

Some of the side stories did take a bit longer than I would have preferred, but the depth they added to the world and characters made it worthwhile. In the end, the payoff was huge, and I gladly forgive the slower parts for the sake of such a strong and emotional finale.

This series deserves much more recognition. Blood of Zeus is a powerful blend of mythology, artistry, and storytelling. A must-watch for fans of ancient epics and animated excellence.

comment_text:
My review>>

Spectacular animation entitled BLOOD OF ZEUS, maintaining the high level of spectacularity, of the story of Heron and Seraphim, sons of Zeus, the animation is so beautiful and vivid to see the lines that it is a pleasure to see the good guys, the bad guys, the gods, the Olympians, and masterfully portraying the titans like Cronos and company!

Aphrodite and Hera are done with such perfection of lines and expressions that you are able to be hypnotized by how well done it is!

I was already a fan of the first seasons, and they managed to maintain the high level of this third one, I really liked other atmospheres that they showed, I loved the underworld scene, the tricks with the boatman!

So friends, if you want to see a well-made animation for adults, and for those who like Greek mythology and its gods, I recommend it with my eyes closed!

In season 3 I thought it was ideal, each episode is 25 minutes long, very dynamic and intense in terms of immersion in the stories

Congratulations on the work of all the staff, authors and directors, Netflix and company... I love what you do with all my heart! This is real cartoons, thank you, Alex Strunz

❌spoiler for the final season ❌

-
-
My only critique for this season is that the fights this season didn’t really feel threatening, because Death isn’t really a permanent thing in this world at all.


We see even human beings like gorgo get to just ‘come back’ in the end.

A better ending in my opinion would be seraphim joining his mother and brother and gorgo in the fields, not just him ‘visiting’.

Like season 2 was so powerful because Zeus died. One of the most powerful gods had fallen….nevermind he came back in the end. So did gorgo a human being whose original body would have decade to bones by now.

The only people who permanently died were gods, and the ones who got thrown down that hole. So I don’t really get the point of season 2, which focuses on zeuses death when he can just…come back

video_title: Blood of Zeus S3 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Just finished watching and I quite enjoyed it. Not gonna say it's perfect as there were certain parts that were clearly rushed and not expanded further but still a good enough conclusion from where it left off. With Netflix being Netflix, the staff did a respectable job cramming all the content meant to last for two more seasons into this final one with all the budget restraints and just 8 episodes of around 25 minutes (the last being around 31 at least). Loved the development and interactions between the main characters. It might be just me but the animation also felt improved compared to the previous seasons and of course the voice actors did an amazing job. The soundtrack being a good fit as always too. With everything said, I'm just glad to see this show getting to properly end and not outright cancelled like various others. Kudos to the Creators and looking forward to see more works from them in the future.

video_title: Blood of Zeus S3 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
These animated movies that change too much and distort ancient stories are inaccurate and nonsensical.

Greek mythology is connected to ancient Greek religion. According to the ancient stories, Zeus was immortal and undefeated. He saved his siblings from a domineering father in the titanomachy, created a new, just world order and ended the cycle of sons overthrowing their fathers. 

The Olympian deities defeated the giants in the Gigantomachy. Zeus also fought Typhon and defeated him. These events happened one after another and had meaning. 

Having Zeus killed, modifying ridiculously the stories and bringing back Cronus are inconsistent arbitrary retellings showing the modern misunderstanding and misinterpretation of ancient stories, ancient cultures, and religions.
